name: security-incident-response
description: Post-breach security incident response — detect / triage / contain / eradicate / recover / postmortem (NIST CSF simplified). OMO-augmented with security-research mode + oracle + websearch + review-work.

positive_triggers.input: Our monitoring flagged 500 failed login attempts from one IP in 10 minutes — should we be worried?
positive_triggers.reason: Detection signal (brute force attempt) → Triage phase (severity + scope)

positive_triggers.input: A user emailed saying their account got compromised and they see orders they didn't place
positive_triggers.reason: User-reported breach → Detect + Triage + Contain + Eradicate full flow

positive_triggers.input: We just got a CVE advisory — our production uses the affected library. Has it been exploited?
positive_triggers.reason: Third-party CVE disclosure → Detect + Triage (verify if exploited)

negative_triggers.input: Add a new REST endpoint that accepts user-uploaded files
negative_triggers.reason: App-layer preventive (file upload validation) → security-and-hardening, NOT incident response

negative_triggers.input: I'm adding the new npm package 'lodash-utils' — should I be worried?
negative_triggers.reason: Supply-chain preventive (dep add) → security-devsecops, NOT incident response

negative_triggers.input: Our p95 latency spiked 10x — what's wrong?
negative_triggers.reason: Performance issue, not security incident → performance-optimization or observability-and-instrumentation

behavioral_evals.scenario: User reports 'A user emailed saying their account got compromised and they see orders they didn't place'
expected_behavior: Agent enters Detect phase: timestamps + freezes evidence + creates .planning/<id>/incident-<id>.md (separate from compromised system logs)
Agent does NOT immediately reset the affected account (would destroy evidence / prevent further attacker observation)
Agent enters Triage phase: classifies severity (Major if single-account, Critical if multi-account compromise suspected) + scope assessment (PII / payment data at risk?)
Agent uses oracle agent for severity / scope / notification timing decisions
Agent enters Contain phase: freezes the affected account + revokes all session tokens + forces password reset + checks for lateral movement
Agent enters Eradicate phase: investigates root cause (credential leak? session hijack? CVE in auth flow?) + patches if root cause is code
Agent invokes /security-research mode for post-incident PoC verification that root cause is fully patched
Agent enters Recover phase: resets all related credentials (not just the affected user) + enhances monitoring + verifies service normal
Agent enters Postmortem phase: writes 5-whys + action items + lessons to .planning/<id>/incident-<id>-postmortem.md
Agent does NOT touch application code unrelated to the root cause (separate skill — incremental-implementation)

behavioral_evals.scenario: User reports 'A CVE was disclosed for one of our dependencies — log4shell-style critical — has it been exploited?'
expected_behavior: Agent enters Detect phase: searches for IOCs (indicators of compromise) via websearch MCP — public exploit code, attack campaigns
Agent checks own logs for suspicious patterns matching the exploit signature
Agent enters Triage phase: classifies based on observed exploitation vs theoretical risk
Agent enters Contain phase: if exploited, isolates affected systems; if not exploited but vulnerable, applies WAF rule or rate limit as temporary mitigation
Agent enters Eradicate phase: upgrades dependency to patched version; security-research mode verifies no exploit possible
Agent enters Recover phase: deploys patched version via blue-green or canary; rolls back if issues
Agent enters Postmortem phase: documents why the unpatched version was in production (lack of automated SCA? skipped security-devsecops process?) + adds action items to prevent recurrence

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker. This skill is post-breach only; preventive concerns route to security-and-hardening (app-layer) or security-devsecops (supply chain + deployment).
